description:
The REAL fix for CF2 (the other arm of the partition-or-gate fork). DEFERRED pending design discussion — not a blocker for the Tier-1 capstone; CF2a gates the hazard in the meantime.

Goal: make --max-concurrency > 1 correct, not just warned. Two candidate designs (decision pending):
  (a) Collision-free partition: group sites by module, one async_stream per module-group, serial WITHIN a group so two workers never touch the same module / interleave its compile-load-restore. Cheaper; still shares one global ExUnit.Server/:cover across groups, so needs care (or per-group cover scoping).
  (b) Per-mutant process isolation: each mutant compiles+restores+tests under its own BEAM node or isolated cover+ExUnit server. The true unlock for >1 core; composes with F2 toward O(S*k/cores). Large architectural change — the moduledoc already flags it out of scope.

Do NOT simply raise the default concurrency — it breaks the byte-identity gate.

## Done (when un-deferred)
- A chosen design lands; --max-concurrency > 1 produces byte-identical classification + correct coverage on a REAL backend (not just fakes); the CF2a experimental warning is removed.
title: CF2b: Module-partitioned safe parallelism (DEFERRED — design fork)
